surface.name: NVIDIA NIM API Catalog hosted quickstart (Developer Program API key + Python)
surface.selection_basis: The official API Catalog Quickstart Guide documents hosted NIM on NVIDIA DGX Cloud via build.nvidia.com: select meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct, Get API Key (joins the Developer Program / NVIDIA account), copy the key, paste the model-page Python sample, replace the API-key placeholder, and run it. LLM APIs reference documents the same catalog surface as POST https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1/chat/completions. Run NIM Anywhere states Developer Program members get free hosted NIM API access for prototyping. No local GPU or CUDA install is required.

alternatives_considered.name: API Catalog web Preview UI only
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The same quickstart documents Preview chat in the browser, but first developer API success is the authenticated Python request after Get API Key.

alternatives_considered.name: Self-hosted NIM containers / Run NIM Anywhere local deploy
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Container pull and local or cloud GPU hosting is a later path after hosted catalog prototyping.

alternatives_considered.name: CUDA Toolkit Ubuntu install through nbody
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Local GPU toolkit path; not the hosted NIM API Catalog onboarding documented for first API calls.

alternatives_considered.name: Hugging Face Serverless NIM endpoints
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Peer prototyping route that requires a Hugging Face account instead of the NVIDIA API Catalog Get API Key flow.

entry_point.developer_intent: Join the NVIDIA Developer Program (or sign in with an NVIDIA account), obtain an API Catalog API key from build.nvidia.com, and receive a hosted NIM chat-completions response.
assumed_prior_state: No NVIDIA Developer Program membership, NVIDIA account session for the API Catalog, or NVIDIA Developer API key is assumed.
A browser and a Python environment able to run the model-page sample are available; no local NVIDIA GPU is assumed.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Run the API Catalog Python sample against a hosted NIM
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated request using the API Catalog key returns chat-completions model output from a hosted NIM (API Catalog quickstart example: meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct) via POST https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1/chat/completions.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Python sample completes and the response includes model-generated text for the requested prompt.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The API Catalog Quickstart documents Get API Key, Developer Program / NVIDIA account signup or sign-in, Copy Key, Copy Code, replace API_KEY_REQUIRED_IF_EXECUTING_OUTSIDE_NGC, and run the Python sample. LLM APIs reference documents URL https://integrate.api.nvidia.com and Endpoint POST /v1/chat/completions for catalog models including meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The first meaningful hosted NIM success is an authenticated API response after Get API Key. The official quickstart's primary non-UI path is the model-page Python sample; a local GPU and CUDA Toolkit are not required. Developer Program membership provides free hosted NIM API access for prototyping per Run NIM Anywhere.

prerequisites.requirement: An NVIDIA account with Developer Program membership created or confirmed through the Get API Key signup flow on build.nvidia.com.
An NVIDIA Developer / API Catalog API key created via Get API Key and stored securely.
A Python environment able to run the model-page sample copied from build.nvidia.com. No local NVIDIA GPU or CUDA Toolkit is required for hosted endpoints.

primary_path.action: Open build.nvidia.com, search for llama-3, and open the meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct model page (acknowledge third-party model warnings if shown).
details: The API Catalog Quickstart uses llama-3.1-8b-instruct as the example; any catalog model follows the same Get API Key flow.
primary_path.input: Intent to call a hosted NIM API
primary_path.output: Model page with Preview and Get API Key controls
primary_path.success_signal: The model page shows Get API Key and a code pane.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Catalog navigation or model availability may change; pick another catalog model if needed.

primary_path.action: Click Get API Key, enter email, and complete NVIDIA account creation or sign-in (joining the Developer Program).
details: Existing NVIDIA accounts sign in with the associated email.
New accounts set a password and follow on-screen account setup.
Signing up through this flow joins the Developer Program.
primary_path.output: Authenticated Developer Program session on the model page
primary_path.success_signal: The flow redirects back to the model page ready to show an API key.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account recovery follows NVIDIA sign-in; exact signup form fields are not fully enumerated in the quickstart.

primary_path.action: Copy the new API key from the popup and store it securely.
details: The quickstart warns to paste the key somewhere safe.
primary_path.success_signal: The key is copied to secure local storage.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the value is lost, generate a new key from the catalog settings or Get API Key flow.

primary_path.action: Click Copy Code on the model page, paste the Python sample into an editor, and replace API_KEY_REQUIRED_IF_EXECUTING_OUTSIDE_NGC with the API key.
details: Optionally change the sample prompt from the documented limerick example.
primary_path.input: API key and model-page Python sample
primary_path.output: Runnable Python script with the API key set
primary_path.success_signal: The placeholder string is replaced with the real key.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing package dependencies for the sample client fail at import or request time.

primary_path.action: Run the Python sample so it sends an authenticated chat-completions request to the hosted NIM endpoint.
details: LLM APIs reference documents base URL https://integrate.api.nvidia.com and Endpoint POST /v1/chat/completions for catalog LLM models including meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct.
The NIM is hosted by NVIDIA on DGX Cloud per the API Catalog Quickstart.
primary_path.input: Configured Python sample and API key
primary_path.success_signal: The request returns a successful response body with model-generated text.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid key, rate limits, or entitlement errors prevent a successful completion.

primary_path.action: Confirm the response includes model-generated text for the prompt.
primary_path.output: Verified first hosted NIM API success
primary_path.success_signal: Model output text is present in the successful response.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An error body means the first API call did not succeed.

branches.condition: The developer prefers another API Catalog model instead of llama-3.1-8b-instruct.
branches.path: Select any catalog model and follow the same Get API Key and Copy Code flow; the quickstart states the same guide applies to any model in the catalog.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Same hosted NIM boundary; model id in the request changes.

branches.condition: The developer already has an NVIDIA Developer API key.
branches.path: Skip Get API Key signup and jump to copying the model-page Python sample (quickstart step 4).
branches.effect_on_first_success: Shortens account creation; credential and execute steps remain.

branches.condition: The developer prefers raw HTTP against the documented LLM API instead of the model-page Python sample.
branches.path: POST to https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1/chat/completions with Authorization Bearer and a JSON body naming a catalog model such as meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct, per the LLM APIs reference.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Same chat-completions endpoint; client tooling differs from the quickstart Python sample.

friction_gates.description: Get API Key requires email and NVIDIA account signup or sign-in, which joins the Developer Program.
A secret API Catalog key must be copied and stored safely before the Python sample can authenticate.
Hosted NIM requests must present the API key; wrong entitlement or role can block hosted endpoint use.

sources.title: API Catalog Quickstart Guide
sections_used: Interacting with NIM through the API Endpoints
Interacting through the web based UI
evidence_supported: build.nvidia.com catalog entry and meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct example
Get API Key joins Developer Program / NVIDIA account
Copy Key and Copy Code Python sample with API_KEY_REQUIRED_IF_EXECUTING_OUTSIDE_NGC
Hosted NIM on NVIDIA DGX Cloud

sources.title: LLM APIs
evidence_supported: meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct listed among catalog LLM models

sources.title: Run NIM Anywhere
evidence_supported: Hosted preview APIs via API Catalog account
Developer Program free access to NIM API endpoints for prototyping
Self-host and Hugging Face routes as alternatives

sources.title: NVIDIA Developer Program
evidence_supported: NIM listed among developer program tools

sources.title: NVIDIA API Catalog (build.nvidia.com)
evidence_supported: Public catalog entry point for hosted NIM APIs

uncertainties.question: What exact current signup fields (identity, legal, marketing preferences) does Get API Key show for a brand-new NVIDIA account?
uncertainties.impact: Signup is treated as one opaque account step; field-level action counts cannot be verified from the quickstart alone.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The API Catalog Quickstart shows email entry and password setup for new accounts but does not enumerate every required form field.

uncertainties.question: Which exact client library and package pins does the live model-page Copy Code sample currently emit?
uncertainties.impact: Configure/execute may require installing whatever client the pane currently copies; the quickstart only documents replacing the API key placeholder and running the code.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The quickstart instructs Copy Code from the build.nvidia.com pane without publishing a fixed snippet on the docs page.

excluded_after_success: CUDA Toolkit / local GPU sample paths
